draft.introduction: Surah Al-Waqi'a ("The Inevitable Event") describes the Day of Judgment and divides humanity into three groups: the foremost in faith, the companions of the right, and the companions of the left. The Prophet reportedly said whoever recites it nightly will never be touched by poverty.

translation.text: When the Inevitable Event comes to pass—no soul will deny its happening—bringing low, raising high. When the earth is shaken with a violent shaking, and the mountains are crumbled to pieces, becoming scattered dust. And you become three kinds: the companions of the right—what are the companions of the right? And the companions of the left—what are the companions of the left? And the foremost are the foremost—those are the ones brought near, in the Gardens of Bliss.
translation.original_text: إِذَا وَقَعَتِ الْوَاقِعَةُ لَيْسَ لِوَقْعَتِهَا كَاذِبَةٌ خَافِضَةٌ رَّافِعَةٌ

translator_notes.note: The tripartite division—al-sabiqun (the foremost), ashab al-yamin (companions of the right), and ashab al-shimal (companions of the left)—differs from the simple binary of saved and damned found in many traditions. The Sufis identified the sabiqun with the saints (awliya) who have attained proximity to God through spiritual purification.
